Command your agents.

A tmux-based grid for orchestrating AI agents from your terminal. Eight panes. One window. Total visibility.

Add to ChromeView source

You’re running six agents. You can’t see any of them.

Every agent buries its output in a separate terminal tab. You alt-tab between them, lose context, miss errors. By the time you notice one agent stalled, three others went off the rails.

One window. Every agent.

researcher

scanning 12 repos for auth patterns...

backend

writing convex/schema.ts migration...

frontend

waiting for design tokens

qa-tester

running 48 integration tests...

docs

generating API reference from types...

deployer

queued behind qa-tester

reviewer

reviewing PR #34 diff (1,204 lines)...

monitor

all systems nominal, 0 errors

The 2×4 tmux grid — each pane runs a live agent session.

01

Visual Agent Grid

Eight panes, one window. Every agent visible at a glance — no tab switching, no context lost.

02

Real-time Output

Watch agents think, write, and build simultaneously. Stdout streams live in every pane.

03

One-Command Dispatch

Send any skill to any pane with a single command. Agents begin working in seconds.

04

Session Persistence

Detach and reattach without losing state. Your agents keep running while you step away.

Start commanding.

Works with Claude Code. That’s it.

npx tab-commander init
Add to Chrome
Tab Commander
